code_snippet:
def test_sql_injection():
    payload = "' OR '1'='1"
    r = client.get(f'/api/search?q={payload}')
    assert r.status_code != 500
    assert 'admin' not in r.text
description: Security SQL injection testing: tests SQL injection payload in search parameter does not expose admin data or cause server errors.
Security XSS testing: verifies stored XSS payloads are sanitized before persisting and returning user comment content.
Security CSRF testing: tests CSRF protection blocks state-changing requests submitted without valid anti-CSRF token.

code_snippet:
def test_password_hashing():
    user = db.create_user('test', 'P@ssw0rd!')
    assert user.password != 'P@ssw0rd!'
    assert bcrypt.checkpw(b'P@ssw0rd!', user.password.encode())
description: Security password testing: checks user passwords are stored as bcrypt hashes rather than plaintext in the database.

code_snippet:
def test_jwt_expiry():
    token = create_token(user_id=1, exp=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1))
    r = client.get('/api/profile', headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'})
    assert r.status_code == 401
description: Security JWT testing: tests expired JWT tokens are rejected with 401 Unauthorized on protected API endpoints.
Security brute force testing: verifies account lockout activates after repeated failed login attempts exceeding threshold.

code_snippet:
def test_path_traversal():
    r = client.get('/api/files?path=../../etc/passwd')
    assert r.status_code in (400, 403)
    assert 'root:' not in r.text
description: Security path traversal testing: tests path traversal attack attempting to read system files is blocked by input validation.

code_snippet:
def test_https_redirect():
    r = client.get('http://example.com/login', allow_redirects=False)
    assert r.status_code == 301
    assert r.headers['Location'].startswith('https://')
description: Security HTTPS testing: checks HTTP requests to sensitive endpoints are redirected to HTTPS protocol.

code_snippet:
def test_security_headers():
    r = client.get('/')
    assert 'X-Content-Type-Options' in r.headers
    assert 'X-Frame-Options' in r.headers
    assert 'Content-Security-Policy' in r.headers
description: Security headers testing: verifies security response headers are present on all pages to prevent common web attacks.

code_snippet:
def test_session_fixation():
    old_session = client.cookies.get('session_id')
    client.post('/api/login', json=creds)
    new_session = client.cookies.get('session_id')
    assert old_session != new_session
description: Security session testing: tests session ID is regenerated after successful login to prevent session fixation attacks.
Security privilege testing: verifies regular users cannot escalate privileges by modifying their own role assignment.
Security input validation testing: tests input length validation rejects excessively large payload fields to prevent DoS attacks.
Security OAuth testing: checks OAuth callback validates state parameter to prevent CSRF in authorization flow.
Security data masking testing: verifies credit card numbers are masked in application logs and never stored in plaintext.
Security file upload testing: tests file upload rejects executable file types and validates MIME type against allowlist.
Security rate limiting testing: checks authentication endpoint rate limiting prevents credential stuffing attack patterns.

code_snippet:
def test_encryption_at_rest():
    db.insert('secrets', {'key': 'api_key', 'value': encrypt('secret123')})
    raw = db.raw_query('SELECT value FROM secrets WHERE key="api_key"')
    assert raw != 'secret123'
description: Security encryption testing: tests sensitive data is encrypted at rest and not stored in readable plaintext format.
Security CORS testing: verifies CORS policy does not reflect arbitrary origins from malicious cross-origin requests.

code_snippet:
def test_mfa_enforcement():
    r = client.post('/api/login', json={'user': 'admin', 'pass': 'correct'})
    assert r.json()['mfa_required'] is True
    assert 'token' not in r.json()
description: Security MFA testing: tests multi-factor authentication is required for admin accounts before issuing access tokens.
